# function: pluscode_encode

Returns the [Plus code](https://maps.google.com/pluscodes/) (Open Location Code) for `(latitude, longitude)` at the requested `length`. Code length controls cell size:

| length | cell size |
| ---: | --- |
| 2 | 1° × 1° (~110 km) |
| 4 | 0.05° × 0.05° (~5.5 km) |
| 6 | ~275 m |
| 8 | ~14 m |
| 10 | ~3.5 m (default) |
| 11 | ~70 cm |

`length` must be even between 2 and 10, or any value 11–15. `latitude` must be in `[-90, 90]`, `longitude` in `[-180, 180]`.

```
pluscode_encode(37.7749, -122.4194, 10)
→ "849VQHFJ+X6"
```

## Example Usage

```terraform
// Encode (latitude, longitude) into a Plus code (Open Location Code). Length 10 (default in upstream OLC) gives ~3.5 m precision.
output "civic_center_sf" {
  value = provider::burnham::pluscode_encode(37.7749, -122.4194, 10)
  // → e.g. "849VQHFJ+X6"
}

// Length must be even between 2 and 10, or any value 11–15.
output "high_precision" {
  value = provider::burnham::pluscode_encode(37.7749, -122.4194, 11)
}
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
pluscode_encode(latitude number, longitude number, length number) string
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `latitude` (Number) Latitude in degrees, [-90, 90].
1. `longitude` (Number) Longitude in degrees, [-180, 180].
1. `length` (Number) Code length: even in [2, 10], or any value in [11, 15]. Out-of-range values are an error.
